If holding for service, keep waffles uncovered on a wire rack in a 160°C oven for no more than 15 min.
Chantilly, compote, and maple syrup portioned separately and applied at the pass.
note: Folded egg whites are non-negotiable for a light, crisp waffle — do not skip. Rest batter 5 min before cooking to allow baking powder to activate. Yield assumes approx. 120–130 ml batter per waffle and a standard Belgian iron.

name: Creamy Basil Pesto Pasta Salad
subtitle: Fusilli · Basil Pesto · Bocconcini · Pine Nuts · Parmesan
label: Pesto Pasta Salad

ingredients.item: Basil Pesto — house or quality purchased

ingredients.prep: loosens and enriches the pesto dressing
toasted in dry pan until golden, cooled
leaves picked, larger leaves torn — finish only

method_steps: PASTA: Cook in a large pot of heavily salted boiling water (water should taste seasoned) until al dente — typically 1 min less than packet time. Drain well. Immediately toss hot pasta with EVOO to prevent sticking. Spread on a tray and cool completely to room temperature.

PESTO CREAM: Whisk pesto, cream, and lemon juice together until smooth. Season with salt and white pepper. Consistency should coat a spoon — loosen with a touch more cream if needed. Refrigerate until service.
COMBINE: Within 1 hour of service, toss cooled pasta with pesto cream until evenly coated. Fold in bocconcini gently.
FINISH: At service, scatter toasted pine nuts, torn basil, and parmesan shavings across the top. Do not mix in — keep as a visible garnish.
service: Serve at room temperature or lightly chilled (no colder than 15°C — cold dulls the pesto flavour).
Combine pesto cream and pasta within 1 hour of service only — pasta dressed too early will absorb dressing and dry out.
Pine nuts, basil, and parmesan applied at the pass to maintain texture and colour.
note: Do not dress pasta ahead of time. Pesto oxidises and loses colour — combine no more than 1 hour before service. Bocconcini should be at room temperature before folding in — cold bocconcini seizes the dressing.

name: Moroccan Couscous Salad
subtitle: Couscous · Dried Apricot & Sultana · Herbs · Harissa Dressing · Toasted Almonds
label: Moroccan Couscous

ingredients.prep: boiling — use vegetable stock to keep vegan
toasted at 170°C for 5–6 min until golden, cooled

method_steps: COUSCOUS: Place couscous in a deep tray or bowl. Pour boiling vegetable stock over, stir once, cover tightly with cling film or a lid. Rest 5 min. Uncover and fluff thoroughly with a fork, breaking up any clumps. Stir in olive oil and season with salt. Spread on a tray to cool to room temperature.
DRESSING: Whisk lemon juice, EVOO, harissa, cumin, and cinnamon together until combined. Season with salt and pepper. Taste — dressing should be bold and slightly sharp.
COMBINE: Toss cooled couscous with dressing, dried apricots, sultanas, most of the parsley, mint, and spring onion. Mix well and check seasoning. Refrigerate if making ahead.
FINISH: At service, scatter toasted almonds and remaining fresh herbs over the top. Do not mix almonds through — keep on top for crunch.
service: Serve at room temperature or lightly chilled.
If made ahead, loosen with an extra squeeze of lemon juice and a drizzle of EVOO before service — couscous absorbs dressing overnight.
Toasted almonds applied at service only to maintain crunch.
note: Best made the day prior — flavours develop significantly overnight. Vegan confirmed: use vegetable stock only. Cinnamon quantity reduced to ½ tsp to avoid overpowering the harissa. Almonds must stay off the salad until service.

name: Pumpkin, Feta & Pepita Salad
subtitle: Roast Pumpkin · Danish Feta · Toasted Pepitas · Honey Lemon Dressing · Mixed Leaves
label: Pumpkin Feta Salad

ingredients.prep: toasted in dry pan until lightly popped, cooled

method_steps: PUMPKIN: Toss diced pumpkin with olive oil, cinnamon, smoked paprika, and salt until evenly coated. Spread in a single layer on lined oven trays — do not overcrowd. Roast at 200°C (fan-forced) for 25–30 min, turning once at 15 min, until edges are golden and caramelised. Cool to room temperature on trays before assembling.
DRESSING: Whisk honey, lemon juice, Dijon, and a pinch of salt until combined. Slowly whisk in EVOO to emulsify. Adjust seasoning.
ASSEMBLE: Spread mixed leaves across the base of the platter or bowl. Arrange roast pumpkin over leaves. Crumble feta across, scatter pepitas.
DRESS: Drizzle dressing over the assembled salad at the point of service — not before.
service: Dress at the point of service only — leaves wilt rapidly once dressed.
Pumpkin can be roasted the day prior and held in coldroom; bring to room temperature before assembling.
Pepitas applied last to preserve crunch.
For buffet service, keep dressing in a separate jug on the side.
note: Do not dress ahead. Roast pumpkin is an excellent prep-ahead component — roast the day before and hold in coldroom. Dice size increased to 3 cm (from 2 cm) to hold shape through roasting and avoid mushiness. Pepitas must go on at service.

name: Traditional Garden Salad
subtitle: Mixed Leaves · Shaved Vegetables · House Vinaigrette
label: Garden Salad

ingredients.prep: peeled, shaved on mandoline or peeler into long ribbons
topped and tailed, halved on the bias

method_steps: VINAIGRETTE: Whisk Dijon, vinegar, honey, and a pinch of salt together until combined. Slowly drizzle in EVOO while whisking continuously to form a stable emulsion. Season with salt and pepper. Store in a sealed jar or squeeze bottle — shake well before use.
PREP: All vegetables must be completely dry before assembly. Spin leaves in batches if needed. Pat capsicum dry. Hold prepped veg separately in coldroom until service.
ASSEMBLE: Combine leaves and spinach as the base. Scatter shaved carrot, capsicum julienne, snow peas, and spring onion evenly across the top.
DRESS: Toss with vinaigrette immediately before service. Season lightly after dressing.
service: Dress at service only — pre-dressed salad wilts within 10–15 min.
For buffet service, present vinaigrette in a separate jug or squeeze bottle for self-service.
All vegetables must be bone-dry before dressing — any water breaks the emulsion and dilutes flavour.
note: Vinaigrette yield approx. 130 ml — sufficient for 20 portions dressed to coat, not drench. Prep leaves and vegetables separately in coldroom and combine only at service. Snow peas can be blanched briefly (30 sec, iced) for a slightly more tender result if preferred.

name: Creamy Potato, Bacon & Herb Salad
subtitle: Waxy Potato · Crispy Bacon · Herb Mayo · Chives
label: Potato Bacon Salad
method_steps: POTATOES: Place whole unpeeled potatoes in a pot of cold well-salted water. Bring to the boil, then reduce to a steady simmer. Cook until just tender — a skewer meets slight resistance in the centre, approx. 20–25 min depending on size. Do not boil hard or the skins split. Drain and cool 10 min.
SLICE: While still warm, slice kipflers on the bias into 1.5 cm rounds, or halve desiree. Skin on throughout.

BACON: Lay rashers flat on lined oven trays. Bake at 200°C for 12–15 min until deeply golden and crispy. Cool on a wire rack, then crumble into rough pieces. Do not press into small crumbs — keep texture.
DRESSING: Whisk mayonnaise, sour cream, vinegar, and Dijon together until smooth. Season generously with salt and pepper — dressing should be well-seasoned as it will be absorbed by the potatoes.

COMBINE: While potatoes are still warm (not hot), toss with dressing until evenly coated — warm potatoes absorb dressing for better flavour. Fold in spring onion and most of the chives and parsley. Taste and adjust seasoning.
FINISH: Scatter remaining fresh herbs and crumbled bacon across the top at service. Do not mix bacon through ahead of time.
service: Serve at room temperature — ideally within 4 hours of dressing.
Dress potatoes while warm for maximum flavour absorption, then hold at room temperature or lightly chilled (above 10°C).
Bacon applied at service only to maintain crispness — pre-mixed bacon will go soft.
note: Kipfler gives the best waxy texture and holds shape well when sliced. Desiree is a reliable backup. Do not overcook — potato must hold its shape once sliced. Bacon quantity converted from '20 rashers' to approx. 400 g for accurate prep. Dress warm, serve at room temp.

name: Spinach & Ricotta Pastizzi Platter
subtitle: Maltese Pastizzi · Spinach & Ricotta · Warm
label: Pastizzi Platter

ingredients.prep: frozen or fresh from quality supplier

method_steps: BAKE: Arrange pastizzi on lined baking trays with space between each — do not crowd. Bake from frozen at 190°C (conventional oven) for 22–25 min until pastry is deep golden, puffed, and crisp. If baking fresh, reduce time to 15–18 min. Check base — it should be firm, not soft.
REST: Remove from oven and rest on tray for 3 min before plating — filling retains significant heat and can burn.
PLATE: Arrange on a lined platter or board. Finish with a very light scatter of flaked sea salt. Place lemon wedges around the edge of the platter.
service: Serve warm. If holding, transfer to kick oven at 70°C on a wire rack — maximum 20 min hold before quality drops.
For longer events, bake in batches rather than holding all pieces — pastry softens on a platter within 15–20 min.
Lemon wedges on the side; do not squeeze over platter pre-service.
note: Pastizzi cool and soften quickly once plated — batch baking and staggered service is strongly recommended for events over 30 min. Supplier quality is critical. Do not refreeze and rebake.

name: Mixed Box Hot Nibbles
subtitle: Spring Rolls · Dim Sims · Cocktail Sausage Rolls · Arancini
label: Hot Nibbles Box

ingredients.prep: dipping sauce — for dim sims and spring rolls

method_steps: SEQUENCE: Stagger cooking so all items are hot simultaneously. Start sausage rolls first (longest cook), then fry items. Target a 5-min window where all items finish together.
SAUSAGE ROLLS: Place on a lined tray. Bake at 190°C (conventional oven) for 15–18 min until pastry is golden and base is firm. Do not fry.
SPRING ROLLS: Fry in oil preheated to 180°C for 3–4 min until deep golden and crispy. Drain on a wire rack — not paper towel, which traps steam and softens the base.
DIM SIMS: Fry at 175°C for 4–5 min until golden, or steam in a bamboo steamer over boiling water for 8–10 min. Do not mix methods on the same batch. Drain well.
ARANCINI: Fry at 180°C for 3–4 min until golden and heated through. Alternatively bake at 200°C for 12–15 min, turning once — spray lightly with oil before baking for colour.
HOLD: Transfer all cooked items to lined trays on a wire rack in the kick oven at 70°C. Do not stack. Hold for no more than 15 min — crispness deteriorates beyond this.
service: Arrange by type in a lined box or on a platter — keep each item in its own section. Label each type for service.
Dipping sauces in individual ramekins placed alongside or at the edge of the platter.
Serve promptly — fried items lose crispness rapidly. Do not cover platter with cling film or foil as steam will soften pastry.
For longer events, cook in staggered batches of 10 every 15–20 min rather than holding all 40 pieces.
note: Cook each item in the correct medium — spring rolls and arancini fry better than they bake; sausage rolls must bake. Fried and baked items must be cooked on separate trays and in separate equipment. Coordinate timing to bring all items to temperature simultaneously. Sauce quantities standardised to approx. 30 ml per ramekin — adjust ramekin count to event size.
